title: Fix: VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID / VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID not injected into frontend Docker image — ConfigError shown on live site
body:
## Issue description

The deployed live site shows a full-screen **Configuration error** on load:

```
Configuration error
VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID and VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID must be set before starting the application.
Please check your .env file or deployment configuration.
```

### Root cause

Vite replaces `import.meta.env.VITE_*` variables **at build time** (during `npm run build`), not at runtime. The values are statically inlined into the JavaScript bundle that is copied into the nginx image.

The CD workflow step that builds the frontend Docker image (`docker build ... client/`) does **not** pass `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` or `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID` as `--build-arg`, so Vite sees them as `undefined`. The resulting bundle has empty values, and `msalConfigured` in `client/src/auth/msal-config.ts` evaluates to `false`, causing `App.tsx` to render `<ConfigError />` on every page load.

Relevant files:
- `client/src/auth/msal-config.ts` — reads `import.meta.env.VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` / `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`
- `client/src/App.tsx` — renders `<ConfigError />` when `msalConfigured` is `false`
- `client/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build; `RUN npm run build` has no `--build-arg` exposure
- `.github/workflows/cd.yml` (line ~76-94) — `docker build ... client/` has no `--build-arg` flags
- `infra/modules/container-app-frontend.bicep` — the frontend Container App has no env var injection (nginx serves static assets only)

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

The frontend must have `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID` available at Vite build time so they are embedded in the production JavaScript bundle and the app authenticates correctly.

The preferred approach is **runtime env-var injection via an nginx entrypoint script**, which produces a single environment-agnostic Docker image reusable across environments (dev, prod). An alternative is build-time injection via Docker build args, but this produces environment-specific images that must be rebuilt per environment.

### Recommended solution: runtime injection via entrypoint script

1. **`client/Dockerfile`** — add an `entrypoint.sh` that, before starting nginx, uses `sed` or `envsubst` to replace placeholder tokens in `index.html` (or a generated `env-config.js` that is loaded as a `<script>` tag) with actual values from container environment variables (`VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`).
2. **`client/src/auth/msal-config.ts`** (and any other consumers) — if the runtime-injection approach is used, read values from a global `window.__ENV__` object (populated by the injected script) in addition to / instead of `import.meta.env`.
3. **`infra/modules/container-app-frontend.bicep`** — add `env` variables for `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID` so Azure Container Apps injects them into the container at startup.
4. **`infra/main.bicep`** — add `frontendAzureClientId` and `frontendAzureTenantId` parameters and pass them to the frontend module.
5. **`.github/workflows/cd.yml`** — pass `vars.API_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `vars.API_AZURE_TENANT_ID` (already used for the backend) as the new Bicep parameters in the `Deploy infrastructure (Bicep)` step.

### Alternative solution: build-time injection

If environment-specific images are acceptable:
1. **`client/Dockerfile`** — declare `ARG VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `ARG VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`, and set them as `ENV` before `RUN npm run build` so Vite picks them up.
2. **`.github/workflows/cd.yml`** — add `--build-arg VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID=${{ vars.API_AZURE_CLIENT_ID }} --build-arg VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID=${{ vars.API_AZURE_TENANT_ID }}` to the `docker build` command for the frontend image.

## Additional notes

- The backend Container App already receives `AZURE_TENANT_ID` and `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` via `container-app.bicep` env vars and `cd.yml` `azureTenantId` / `azureClientId` Bicep parameters. The frontend needs a parallel mechanism.
- `vars.API_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `vars.API_AZURE_TENANT_ID` are the correct GitHub repository variables to use — they are already referenced in `cd.yml` for the backend Bicep parameters.
- The runtime injection approach aligns with the Docker-first / 12-factor principle: one image, configuration via environment. It avoids rebuilding the image when credentials rotate.
- If runtime injection is chosen, the `window.__ENV__` config object must be populated before React hydrates. A `<script src="/env-config.js">` tag in `index.html` is a common pattern; the entrypoint generates `env-config.js` from container env vars at startup.
- Security: `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID` are public client IDs (not secrets); it is safe to inject them as env vars in the Container App without using Key Vault.
- The `ConfigError` component and `msalConfigured` guard in `App.tsx` should be retained as the correct error-handling path for genuinely misconfigured environments.
- Tests: update or add tests verifying that the config is read correctly from `window.__ENV__` (if using runtime injection) or from `import.meta.env` build args.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The live site no longer shows the **Configuration error** screen on load.
- [ ] `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID` are correctly available to the frontend at runtime in the production Container App.
- [ ] The frontend Container App authenticates users via Azure Entra ID (MSAL redirect flow works end-to-end).
- [ ] If runtime injection is chosen: `client/Dockerfile` includes an entrypoint that injects env vars before nginx starts, and `container-app-frontend.bicep` declares the required env vars.
- [ ] If build-time injection is chosen: `client/Dockerfile` accepts `ARG VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `ARG VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`, and `cd.yml` passes them as `--build-arg` during the frontend Docker build step.
- [ ] `infra/main.bicep` and `container-app-frontend.bicep` are updated so the correct client ID and tenant ID values flow from CD variables through Bicep into the running container.
- [ ] The CD workflow passes `vars.API_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `vars.API_AZURE_TENANT_ID` (already set) to the frontend Container App via the chosen mechanism.
- [ ] Existing CI and CD pipelines pass without modification to secrets or repository variables.
- [ ] Unit/component tests cover the case where config values are present and where they are absent (the `ConfigError` path).
- [ ] No client IDs or tenant IDs are hardcoded in source code or committed to the repository.
